arguments.content:
# Commitments System Schema

This directory tracks your executive commitments, signals, decisions, and delegated items.

## Directory Structure

```
commitments/
├── README.md           # This file
├── open/               # Active commitments requiring attention
├── resolved/           # Completed commitments
├── signals/
│   ├── pending/        # Unprocessed signals from conversations
│   └── expired/        # Stale signals (auto-expire after 24h for executives)
├── decisions/          # Captured decisions with rationale
└── parked-ideas/       # Ideas saved for later review
```

## Signal Schema (signals/pending/*.md)

Signals are raw inputs from conversations that may become commitments.

```yaml
---
type: signal
created_at: 2024-01-15T09:30:00Z
source: conversation
immediacy: realtime|high|medium|low  # realtime = broadcast immediately
expires_at: 2024-01-16T09:30:00Z     # Auto-expire after 24h for executives
destination: commitments/open|decisions|parked-ideas|intelligence
confidence: 0.0-1.0                  # How certain this is actionable
---
Content describing the signal...
```

## Commitment Schema (open/*.md, resolved/*.md)

Commitments are tracked obligations with deadlines and owners.

```yaml
---
type: commitment
created_at: 2024-01-15T09:30:00Z
title: Brief description
status: owned|waiting|escalated
delegated_to: person-name|null      # Who you delegated this to
resolution_path: action-needed|needs_decision|agent-can-handle|null
stale_after: 5d                      # Flag for follow-up after this duration
resolved_by: null                    # Date resolved (set when completed)
priority: critical|high|medium|low
---
Full description and context...

## Progress Notes
- 2024-01-15: Created, delegated to Sarah
- 2024-01-17: Follow-up sent, no response yet
```

## Decision Schema (decisions/*.md)

Record important decisions with rationale for future reference.

```yaml
---
type: decision
created_at: 2024-01-15T09:30:00Z
title: Decision summary
outcome: approved|rejected|deferred
context: Brief context
rationale: Why this decision was made
attendees: Who was present
impacts: What this affects
follow_up: null or date for outcome review
---
Full decision details...
```

## Parked Idea Schema (parked-ideas/*.md)

```yaml
---
type: parked_idea
created_at: 2024-01-15T09:30:00Z
title: Idea summary
resurface_at: 2024-02-15  # Review after this date
category: strategy|product|ops|people
---
Idea details and context...
```

## Commands

- **"show commitments"** - Get digest of all open items
- **"who owes me what?"** - See delegation status
- **"/plan <description>"** - Create structured execution plan

## Executive Defaults

- Signal expiration: 24 hours (you move fast)
- Delegation follow-up: 2 days without update
- Digest: Morning (8am) + Evening (5pm) on weekdays
- Triage: 3x daily (9am, 1pm, 6pm)
- Most commitments are delegations, not personal tasks

arguments.content:
# Open Commitments

Track commitments that are currently open and being worked on.

## Format

Each commitment file should include:
- Title and description
- Status (open/in-progress/blocked)
- Assigned person
- Due date (if any)
- Related signals and decisions

# Resolved Commitments

Archive of completed and resolved commitments.

## Movement

Commitments move here when:
- Task is completed
- Decision is finalized
- Delegation is confirmed done
- Signal expires without action

# Pending Signals

Time-sensitive information requiring executive attention or decision.

## Signal Lifecycle

1. **Captured** - Signal extracted from conversation/email/meeting
2. **Validated** - Confirmed as actionable (within 24h)
3. **Promoted** - Converted to commitment or decision
4. **Expired** - Auto-archived after 24h if no action

## Schema

- `immediacy`: realtime | high | normal
- `expires_at`: ISO timestamp
- `destination`: commitment | decision | parked-idea | intelligence

# Expired Signals

Signals that were not acted upon within the 24h window.

## Review

Executives should review expired signals weekly to identify:
- Systematic bottlenecks
- Recurring signal types that need automation
- Items that should have been escalated

# Decisions Log

Record of all executive decisions with rationale and context.

## Why Track Decisions

- Revisit when circumstances change
- Onboard new team members
- Audit trail for strategic shifts
- Identify decision patterns

## Decision Schema

- `outcome`: what was decided
- `rationale`: why this path
- `context`: who was present, what factors
- `review_date`: when to reassess (if applicable)
- `affected_areas`: teams/projects impacted

# Parked Ideas

Strategic ideas and initiatives deferred for future consideration.

## Resurfacing

Parked ideas are reviewed:
- Weekly: Quick scan for immediate opportunities
- Monthly: Strategic alignment check
- Quarterly: Portfolio review - execute, defer, or discard

## When to Park

- Right idea, wrong timing
- Needs more information
- Dependent on other initiatives
- Low priority vs current focus

Your CEO commitment tracking system is now ready! Here's what I've set up:

## 📋 System Overview

**Triage Mission** (runs 3x daily: 9am, 1pm, 6pm)
- Checks delegated items and follows up after 2 days
- Expires signals older than 24 hours (executives move fast)
- Escalates overdue items
- Routes informational signals to intelligence
- Sends concise alerts for anything needing attention

**Digest Mission** (runs morning 8am + evening 5pm on weekdays)
- Groups commitments by responsibility:
  - **DELEGATED** — items you've assigned to others, with follow-up status
  - **OWNED** — items you need to act on personally, sorted by urgency
  - **DECISIONS PENDING** — items requiring your decision
  - **RECENT DECISIONS** — decisions from last 7 days
- Keeps everything scannable (one-liners, bullets)

## 🎯 How to Use

- **"show commitments"** — Get your full digest anytime
- **"who owes me what?"** — Check delegation status
- **`/plan <description>`** — Create structured execution plans for complex initiatives

I'll automatically:

- Handle agent-can-homework items (PR reviews, drafts, research) with your permission first

- `commitments/README.md` — Full schema documentation
- `commitments/open/` — Active commitments
- `commitments/resolved/` — Completed items
- `commitments/signals/pending/` — Unprocessed signals
- `commitments/decisions/` — Captured decisions
- `commitments/parked-ideas/` — Ideas for later
